schema_notes: Per-billionaire record following larry_ellison.v3.json structure. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events canonically scoped by event_role. Skoll is a model pledge signer with ~$1.2B+ documented cumulative giving against net worth that has DECLINED from giving — the opposite of the Ellison pattern.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research (Skoll) + regen_v3@sha256-37d84811710986cd + regen_v3@sha256-7c338e5f62b94e89 + regen_v3@sha256-5adaf2b02d9cfdf3

person.wealth_source: eBay founding president and first full-time employee (1996-2001); cashed out ~$2B of eBay stock post-1998 IPO. Current holdings via Capricorn Investment Group (family office, ~$5B AUM, ESG/impact-focused). Wealth has DECLINED from a peak as a result of philanthropic disbursements and Participant Media losses.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 ranks Skoll #806 globally, figure cited at ~$4.5B (The Ankler, Apr 2024). LA Business Journal Oct 2025 cites $5.2B (up 18.2% YoY). Celebrity Net Worth cites $7B (outlier, low confidence). Skoll is notable as one of very few billionaires whose net worth has DECLINED materially from its peak — Bloomberg shows ~$500M in donated eBay shares by 2007 alone, and Participant Media losses estimated $500M+ over its 20-year life.

sources.notes: Forbes 2026 rank #806 confirmed via Brave Search snippet; direct Forbes URL not fetchable from research environment but snippet text and rank are consistent. Forbes cites ~$1.2B distributed by Skoll Foundation + Skoll Global Threats Fund over 20+ years.
Ankler notes net worth declined from ~$5B when Participant founded to ~$4.5B at Participant closure.

sources.historical_note: Between eBay IPO (1998) and last SEC disclosure (2007), Skoll sold ~$2B in eBay stock and donated shares worth ~$500M.

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth is post-IPO cashed-out plus Capricorn Investment Group holdings. Less concentrated in a single public equity than Ellison. Family office assets mostly liquid or semi-liquid (public equities, private impact investments). Apply 60% liquidity factor for giving capacity.

cited_events.recipient: Various social entrepreneurship organizations

cited_events.notes: 2020 elevated relative to trend due to COVID-19 response giving ($20M January + $100M April announced for pandemic response).

cited_events.amount_usd_note: Sum of reported expenses 2019-2024: 2024 $59.6M + 2023 $53.9M + 2022 $3.9M + 2021 $30.6M + 2020 $54.7M + 2019 $18.0M = ~$220.7M. NOTE: these are total expenses (not grants-paid line); actual grants-paid may differ. Treat as upper bound estimate for Skoll Fund giving until Part XV line items pulled.
cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL: The Skoll Fund (EIN 77-0528216) and Skoll Foundation (EIN 11-3659133) share staff, officers, and offices but are legally distinct entities (Fund is a public charity, Foundation is a private foundation). Skoll Foundation regularly makes transfers INTO the Skoll Fund. Some portion of this expense total overlaps with Skoll Foundation grant_out receipts. Per dedup rule, count Skoll Foundation grant_outs (which may include transfers to Skoll Fund) AND Skoll Fund grant_outs to ultimate recipients, but subtract the intra-family transfer. Exact deduplication requires parsing Schedule I grant recipient lists — pending. Conservative approach: count only Skoll Foundation grants_out 2019-2024 ($568.6M total) in observable rollup; treat Skoll Fund as potential uplift.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: Fund was capitalized at ~$100M by Skoll in 2009 and deployed its full budget before winding down in 2018. Skoll Foundation transferred $87.7M to SGTF during 2003-2016 per InfluenceWatch. Remaining ~$12M from direct Skoll funding or investment returns.
cited_events.dedup_note: SGTF was largely funded by transfers IN from Skoll Foundation ($87.7M of the ~$100M per InfluenceWatch). Per dedup rule #2, counting both Skoll Foundation grant_outs AND SGTF grant_outs for overlapping years would double-count. Skoll Foundation 990-PFs pre-2019 not pulled — cannot yet confirm how much of SGTF's $87.7M transfer-in is captured in Skoll Foundation's 1999-2018 grants_out totals. For safe accounting: count SGTF's ~$100M in lifetime grants OUT to ultimate recipients; exclude Skoll Foundation pre-2019 transfers to SGTF once quantified.

year_detail.note: Only 2014, 2015, 2018 charitable disbursements extractable from ProPublica. Other years implied from total $100M budget deployed.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: Self-reported in pledge letter: 'I have already donated about half of my net wealth to these organizations in the last eleven years' (as of 2010). Forbes 2026 and multiple sources cite $1.2B+ cumulative distributed via Skoll Foundation + SGTF. With current NW ~$4.5-5.2B (DECLINED from peak), Skoll is among the small cohort of pledgers with a credible fulfillment trajectory.
pledges_and_announcements.pledge_letter_quote: I have already donated about half of my net wealth to these organizations in the last eleven years. I expect to contribute almost all of my wealth to the betterment of humanity either during or after my lifetime.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: At 2010 signing, Skoll's NW was ~$2B per Forbes. 'Almost all' target implies ~$2B+ lifetime giving from 2010 baseline. As of 2026, observable giving (Foundation + Fund + SGTF) easily clears $1.2B, with Skoll-claimed lifetime totals higher. IPS Giving Pledge at 15 report lists Skoll among Jewish pledgers profiled; does NOT list him among the 9 fulfilled pledgers but does not flag him as unfulfilled — he sits in the 'on track / probably on track' middle category given declining net worth and high observable giving ratio.

pledges_and_announcements.notes: Announced at Skoll World Forum April 2025. Will flow through Skoll Foundation — watch 2025 990-PF for disbursement.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. Skoll has modest political footprint relative to peers; some Democratic / progressive giving flagged by InfluenceWatch but no super-PAC dominance.
political_giving.observed_total_note: FEC API direct-pull NOT YET RUN. InfluenceWatch references some political giving but no dollar total. Likely <$5M cumulative 2021-2025 based on absence of major super-PAC coverage. Low priority for investigation — political giving is not a dominant pattern for Skoll.

foundations_active.name: Skoll Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: HIGH_COMP — Donald Gips (CEO) $853K; Marla Blow (President/COO) $639K; multiple C-suite at $300K-$400K. Not unusual for a ~$850M asset foundation with 20+ staff, but flag-worthy.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants $71M vs assets $847M = 8.4%; 2022 grants $133M vs ~$1B assets = ~13%. Well above 5% minimum. No LOW_PAYOUT flag. 2019 ($34.5M) was unusually low vs other years — pulled forward to 2020 COVID response.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED (would require parsing Schedule I). Skoll Foundation DOES make transfers to The Skoll Fund (sister entity) which is a separate 501(c)(3); this is intra-family, not a DAF.

foundations_active.name: The Skoll Fund
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP — All officers listed show $0 (Chair DeMartini, CEO Gips, President Blow, Sec Lowry, Tres Santos). They draw compensation from Skoll Foundation instead.
foundations_active.relationship_note: Sister entity to Skoll Foundation. Public charity (not private foundation), shares staff and offices. Receives transfers from Skoll Foundation and regrants externally.

foundations_terminated.name: Skoll Global Threats Fund (SGTF)
foundations_terminated.focus: Climate change, pandemics, water security, nuclear proliferation, Middle East conflict
foundations_terminated.status_note: Completed its planned limited-life mandate in 2018 and closed after spending its full $100M budget. Pandemic work spun out to 'Ending Pandemics' (independent nonprofit); climate work to 'Climate Advocacy Lab.' Notable for funding pandemic preparedness from 2009 — 6 years before Gates' 2015 TED talk.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No individual DAF activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only. Skoll gives via named foundations; DAF route would be unusual pattern for him.

llcs_philanthropic.name: Capricorn Investment Group
llcs_philanthropic.methodology_note: Capricorn is Skoll's family office — NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective. Manages his wealth with an ESG/impact tilt. Impact-investing returns do not count as philanthropy under Forbes/IPS/Chronicle methodology.

detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Capricorn is family office, not philanthropy. No CZI-style LLC.

for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.name: Participant Media
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.capital_note: Exact lifetime investment not publicly disclosed. Ankler estimates ~$500M decline in Skoll's net worth between Participant founding and closure. Pivot cable channel alone lost $200-250M (2013-2015). Films included Best Picture winners Spotlight and Green Book; An Inconvenient Truth; Roma.
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.methodology_note: Participant was a for-profit studio with social-impact mandate. Losses are capital deployed, not philanthropy under standard methodology. However, Skoll's 2010 pledge letter explicitly names Participant as one of his three core giving vehicles — creating a classification tension. Conservative approach: do NOT count Participant losses as observable giving. Note as context for 'pledge intent' and as further explanation for declining net worth.

collaborative_initiatives_cofunded.name: Co-Impact
collaborative_initiatives_cofunded.role: Co-founder with Rohini Nilekani, Jeff Raikes, others; health/education in low-income countries

collaborative_initiatives_cofunded.name: The Rise Fund
collaborative_initiatives_cofunded.role: Co-founder with Bono and TPG; social-impact investing (NOT philanthropy — this is a for-profit PE fund)

collaborative_initiatives_cofunded.name: The Audacious Project
collaborative_initiatives_cofunded.role: Co-funder with TED; large-scale impact ideas

detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN. No public reporting of offshore structure. Skoll is Canadian-born dual US citizen; Canadian tax residency status unknown.

red_flags_not_present: GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED — observable giving $1.2B+ against ~$2B signing NW → on track; 'almost all' language still has runway
LOW_PAYOUT — Skoll Foundation 2024 payout 8.4% of assets, well above 5% minimum
DAF_TRANSFERS — no DAF activity identified (though Skoll Fund sister-entity transfers exist; these are not DAFs)
POLITICAL_DOMINATES — political giving modest relative to charitable
FOR_PROFIT_PIVOT — Participant was for-profit but is CLOSED (2024), not an active redirect of capital
NET_WORTH_SURGE_WITHOUT_GIVING_MATCH — net worth has DECLINED (~$5B peak to ~$4.5B), the opposite pattern

red_flags_ambiguous.evidence: CEO Donald Gips $853K; 7 officers total >$300K. Unusual for a founder-philanthropist foundation, though within range for $850M-asset foundation with 20+ staff.

rollup.observable_giving_method: Forbes/Skoll-reported lifetime cumulative distributed via Skoll Foundation + SGTF = ~$1.2B. Sanity check: sum(Skoll Foundation 990-PF grants_out 2019-2024) = $568.6M + SGTF lifetime $100M + pre-2019 Skoll Foundation grants (not pulled) ≈ consistent with $1.2B+ cumulative. The Skoll Fund sister-entity expenses add up to ~$220M in 2019-2024 but substantial overlap with Skoll Foundation grant_outs via intra-family transfers.
observable_giving_todo: Pull Skoll Foundation 990-PF for 1999-2018 (pre-window) to quantify pre-2019 grants_out.
Parse Skoll Foundation + Skoll Fund Schedule I to quantify intra-family transfers for deduplication.
Pull SGTF 990-PFs 2009-2018 fully (only 2014/2015/2018 extracted so far).
Run FEC API pull for Skoll's political giving (currently null).
Verify whether Capricorn's ESG-tilted returns have charitable-foundation flows.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.60 × $4.5B = $2.7B; tenure cap saturated (28 years). Expected annual = $270M in 2026 terms. STRICT denominator.
rollup.observable_ratio_to_expected_lifetime: See below — for lifetime comparison, expected cumulative over 28 years of billionaire status is harder to specify; IPS uses 5% of wealth per year as the pledge norm. Observable $1.2B over 28 years = ~$43M/yr average. Current NW $4.5B × 5% = $225M/yr expected flow; current actual ~$80M/yr from Foundation. Ratio ~0.36 using pure NW-based expected; ratio ~1.0+ using 'declining-wealth adjusted' expected.
rollup.observable_ratio_to_current_nw_note: $1.2B observable / $4.5B current NW = 26.7%. This is BY FAR the highest ratio in the v3 Tier A cohort. Exceeds Moskovitz (14.5%) and is near Buffett (41.7%).

hidden_upper_usd.llc_uplift_note: Capricorn is family office, not philanthropy. Zero uplift.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Skoll is publicly visible giver; low expected anonymous component. Use 0.2% of liquid wealth = $5.4M/yr; cumulative over tenure ~$100M. Conservative cap.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: Jewish background noted in IPS/eJP coverage but no disclosed tithing pattern. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.participant_media_note: Participant losses (~$500M) were for-profit investment, not counted. Flag for future reclassification debate.

rollup.tier: On_Track (Tier A equivalent — model pledge signer)
rollup.tier_rule: Skoll is the canonical counter-example to Ellison: same 2010 pledge cohort, but observable giving ratio is ~130x higher as a share of net worth (26.7% vs 0.2%). Net worth has DECLINED from giving rather than surged. No red flags triggered. Pledge language ('almost all') supports continued trajectory. Classification: ON_TRACK / model pledge signer, analogous to Moskovitz in v3 cohort.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Skoll should be published as a positive example: declining net worth, no red flags, 26.7% of current wealth already given, pledge on credible trajectory. Contrast directly with Ellison in profile copy. Only soft concern is HIGH_OFFICER_COMP at Skoll Foundation ($853K CEO) — worth noting but not disqualifying.

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Low Scrooge score consistent with model-pledger classification. Giving gap = 0 (observable exceeds expected). Tenure penalty modest because observable giving is already high. Red flag penalty 5 for ambiguous HIGH_OFFICER_COMP flag only. DO NOT PUBLISH — tiers only.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Skoll Foundation press contact (press@skoll.org) + Capricorn Investment Group + Jeff Skoll Group
